title: halesia carolina var. glabrescens
intro: Halesia carolina var. glabrescens, also known as the Carolina silverbell, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the southeastern United States. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional folk medicine, where it has been used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Halesia carolina var. glabrescens include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to make tea or applied topically as a poultice or salve, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
